series.title: Key & Peele
series.description: Key & Peele is an American sketch comedy television show. It stars Keegan-Michael Key and Jordan Peele, both former cast members of MADtv. Each episode of the show consists of several pre-taped sketches starring the two actors, introduced by Key and Peele in front of a live studio audience.

1.format_desc: Live studio audience with standup intros

2.format_desc: Live studio audience with standup intros

3.format_desc: Live studio audience with standup intros

4.format_desc: Car conversation cold opens, no studio audience

5.format_desc: Car conversation cold opens, no studio audience

sketch_series.obama_luther: Obama & Luther — President Obama with his anger translator Luther
sketch_series.substitute_teacher: Substitute Teacher — Mr. Garvey butchers students' names (A-A-Ron, Jay-Quellin, etc.)
sketch_series.east_west_bowl: East/West Bowl — college football player introductions with absurd names
sketch_series.the_valets: The Valets — two valets from Berkshire Restaurant obsess over celebrities (Liam Neesons, etc.)
sketch_series.meegan_andre: Meegan & André — toxic on-again off-again couple
sketch_series.metta_world_news: Metta World News — NBA player Metta World Peace delivers bizarre news
sketch_series.levi_cedric: Levi & Cedric — two inner-city friends, Levi constantly joins new trends
sketch_series.black_republicans: Black Republicans — group of black men trying to recruit other Black voters to the GOP

episodes.title: Bitch
episodes.overview: Key & Peele airs Wednesdays on Comedy Central. Jordan Peele and Keegan-Michael Key attempt to say a forbidden word in regards to their women, Lil Wayne adjusts to life behind bars, and President Obama expresses how he really feels - with the help of his anger translator.

sketches.title: Phone Call
sketches.description: Key and Peele struggle to say the word 'bitch' when referring to their girlfriends during a phone conversation.

sketches.title: Obama & Luther
sketches.description: President Obama gives a measured response about current events while his anger translator Luther expresses his true feelings.

sketches.title: Lil Wayne in Prison
sketches.description: Lil Wayne tries to adjust to prison life but his wealthy lifestyle and celebrity status make it difficult to fit in.

sketches.title: Gay Wedding Advice
sketches.description: A man seeks relationship advice from his friends about his upcoming gay wedding.

sketches.title: Auction Block
sketches.description: A slave auction is conducted like a modern day auction house with sophisticated bidding procedures.

sketches.description: President Obama and his anger translator Luther engage in a rap battle scenario. This features Obama needing Luther to express his more aggressive thoughts through hip-hop.

sketches.description: Two amateur actors portraying MLK and Malcolm X compete for the affection of a community theater audience, escalating their performances to absurd extremes.

sketches.description: A sketch involving characters giving advice about gay weddings, likely featuring misunderstandings or awkward social situations.
